subject: Unauthorised substance (extract from Griffonia simplicifolia seeds) for sale on web shop from Spain
product_category: Dietetic foods, food supplements and fortified foods
hazards_observed: Following a consumer complaint, we identified a food supplement on the web shop Naturitas.be containing an extract of Griffonia simplicifolia seeds. In Belgium, the competent authority considers extracts of Griffonia simplicifolia containing 5-HTP as novel food. In addition, our national legislation (the Plants Order) restricts the placing on the market of this product as a food: Griffonia simplicifolia is on our list of plants which are not allowed for use in or as foodstuffs. This means that any part, preparation or fruit of those plants must not be consumed as such and must not be used for preparing foodstuffs. It is a forbidden plant in Belgium.

subject: HHC in fruit gums from the Czech Republic
hazards_observed: Cannabinoids, especially synthetically obtained cannabinoids, are considered as novel (literally, the Novel Food Catalogue states: Synthetically obtained cannabinoids are considered as novel). The single substance HHC, a cannabinoid, is considered a novel food. Extracts from Cannabis sativa and other products thereof that contain cannabinoids are considered novel foods, as there is no documented history of consumption for such products. According to Regulation (EU) 2017/2470, neither extracts from Cannabis sativa that (may) contain cannabinoids (here HHC), nor extracts and/or pastes from flowers, leaves or stems, nor the cannabinoid HHC itself are included in the Union list of novel foods. The products found or the HHC contained therein is therefore a non-authorised novel food within the meaning of Art. 3 para. 2a Regulation (EU) 2015/2283.

subject: Too high Selenium content and various labelling violations on parrot feed from Greece and the Netherlands
product: Multiple feed products for ornamental birds
hazards_observed: On the labelling of the products, we read a content of 0.85 mg/kg of selenium (3b802). According to the authorisation for this additive, the maximum permitted level for selenium is 0.50 mg/kg. In addition, multiple labelling deficiencies have been identified: the claim “GMO free” is not authorised, the claim "diet" is only authorised on feeds with a particular nutritional purpose (767/2009, article 18, a), but these feeds do not fall under an authorised particular nutritional purpose (2020/354), and the additives are not correctly declared.
